message:
fix(models): Run model download on separate OS thread to avoid Tokio conflicts

The model download was experiencing panics when reqwest::blocking
attempted to create its own Tokio runtime while being called from
within an existing async context. This manifested as "Cannot start a
runtime from within a runtime" errors during cass models install.

Move the download operation to a separate std::thread::spawn() call
which isolates it from the parent Tokio runtime. This allows
reqwest::blocking to safely create its ephemeral runtime without
conflicting with the main async executor.

Add explicit error handling for thread panics with actionable hint
message directing users to retry or report the panic output.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
